scenario: The installed OpenClaw gateway path proves LCO as a working Codex-first local app: search, describe, expand, approved harmless live Codex control, post-action refresh, and safe agent reasoning from source refs.
surface: installed OpenClaw gateway and local app surfaces
expected_public_safe_evidence: declared and callable loo_* tool count
Codex notification counts and completion status
post-action refresh timestamp and source refs
agent reasoning note from safe summaries only
desktop proof marker when desktop collaboration is claimed
structured desktop act blockers before any unproven GUI mutation path
desktop approval action hash bound to backend, target app, target window, and action when desktop collaboration is claimed
desktop collaboration runtime marker action_hash matching the approved desktop action hash when desktop collaboration is claimed
proof-report runtime marker path for desktop-collaboration-action-bound-v1-1 when desktop collaboration observation is valid
pass_criteria: Installed OpenClaw gateway invokes required read/search/expand tools and records public-safe output.
One harmless Codex action is dry-run first and live second with a matching approval audit id through the same user-facing path.
Session state is refreshed after the live action and the agent can cite source refs without raw transcript reads.
Runtime proof runner accepts the proof packet with zero raw/private evidence findings.
Release claim gates distinguish this working-app proof from broader unproven claims.
fail_criteria: The proof uses only internal CLI helpers while the installed gateway path remains unproven.
Any live action runs without matching approval evidence.
The agent must read raw transcripts to reason about the updated session.
Evidence stores raw prompt text, raw transcript spans, screenshots, SQLite pages, or secrets.
Docs imply unsupported adapter parity, broad desktop control, remote sync, or enterprise readiness.
known_gaps: The runtime proof runner can validate public-safe v1.1 proof markers, but the full installed gateway live-control and post-action refresh loop still needs real runtime evidence.

Desktop collaboration has backend-specific scratch proofs; release status now requires the action-bound runtime marker, proof-report now emits that marker for valid public-safe observations, verifies desktop approval action hashes against backend, target app, target window, and action, binds the runtime marker action_hash to the approved action when desktop GUI collaboration is claimed, and loo_desktop_act returns structured missing-proof blockers while remaining dry-run-only. This is still not a generic product-level action path.
The local Mac search UI has a connected live CLI proof path, but not packaged/signed macOS product UI readiness.
Claude Code remains an adapter inventory item, not an equivalent working path.
next_action: Keep claim-scoped runtime markers tied to their release claims: #158/#159 for Codex working-app proof, #160 only when desktop GUI collaboration is claimed, and #161 only when connected local UI readiness is claimed.
proof_boundary: This local public-safe scorecard defines Milestone 7 working-app proof. It is not proof until runtime evidence from the installed gateway and named local surfaces exists.
